# v1.15.1 Real-iPhone Smoke Checklist

**Phase:** 50 — Wave-3 Real-iPhone Smoke Gate
**Milestone:** v1.15.1 iOS Polish & Drawer Hardening
**Cross-links:** [`50-CONTEXT.md`](./phases/50-wave-3-real-iphone-smoke-gate/50-CONTEXT.md) · [`ROADMAP.md` Phase 50](./ROADMAP.md)
**Status (until tester signs):** Generated by Plan 50-01 Task 1 — all 10 boxes UNCHECKED. Tester edits this file inline at the bedside (Plan 50-01 Task 2).

---

## Why this checklist exists (v1.13 D-12 closure)

This document closes the **v1.13 D-12 deferral**. The PROJECT.md v1.13.0 Validated entry recorded the deferral verbatim:

> "Real-iPhone smoke test (drawer, keyboard, STOP-red contrast in standalone PWA mode) recommended post-deploy as v1.13.1 hotfix fuel — non-blocking per D-12."

v1.13 shipped without bedside iPhone access; v1.15.1 schedules that work explicitly. Per **Phase 49 D-25**, this checklist elevates real-iPhone testing from "recommended post-deploy" to **primary verification surface** for the milestone. Phase 51 / REL-04 will not unblock until SMOKE-01..10 are signed off in the committed copy of this file.

## Why CI cannot replace this checklist (Phase 49 D-24 + PITFALLS.md P-19/P-20)

Playwright e2e (the existing `chromium` project + the new `webkit-iphone` project added in Phase 47) **cannot** emulate any of the surfaces this checklist verifies on real hardware:

- The **iOS soft keyboard** does not appear on focus and does not fire `visualViewport.resize` — see `.planning/research/PITFALLS.md` P-19 + P-20. DRAWER-TEST-03 (`e2e/drawer-visual-viewport.spec.ts`) synthesizes the resize event as a wiring/structural CI proxy only.
- The **Dynamic Island** safe-area insets (`safe-area-inset-top` in portrait, `safe-area-inset-left/right` in landscape) are not painted by Playwright WebKit on Linux.
- **bfcache restore** (the `pageshow.persisted === true` branch DRAWER-03 binds) cannot be triggered headlessly — Phone-app round-trips and lock/wake cycles are not scriptable.
- `apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style: black-translucent` (declared in `src/app.html`) is honored only when launched from the Home Screen icon in standalone PWA mode.

Per **Phase 49 50-CONTEXT D-24**: CI is wiring/structural; Phase 50 is visual/bedside. **This checklist is the primary verification surface for SMOKE-04, SMOKE-05, SMOKE-06, SMOKE-08, and SMOKE-09.**

## Tester instructions (~15 minutes, real iPhone, standalone PWA mode)

- **Device required:** iPhone 14 Pro or newer (Dynamic Island device), iOS 17+ ideally iOS 18+ — covers the iOS 26 `visualViewport.height` post-dismiss regression (Apple Developer Forums #800125, verified by SMOKE-05).
- **Install via Safari, NOT browser-tab testing:** Open the v1.15.1 deploy URL in Safari → tap **Share** → tap **"Add to Home Screen"**. Launch the app from the Home Screen icon (standalone PWA mode). Browser-tab testing does NOT exercise the chrome / safe-area / bfcache code paths and is **INVALID** for this checklist (D-09 + SMOKE-01 expected text).
- **Expected duration:** ~15 minutes of focused bedside-style time per full smoke pass.
- **Hardware keyboard:** SMOKE-07 requires a paired Bluetooth or Smart Connector keyboard. If unavailable, annotate SMOKE-07 as "DEFERRED — no hardware keyboard available" (D-17/D-18) and route per D-08 option 3 (re-test pending). This does NOT block sign-off — 9/10 Pass + 1 DEFERRED-with-route is acceptable for Phase 51 / REL-04.
- **Recording outcomes:** Tick the appropriate **Pass / Fail / DEFERRED / Fail (route to v1.15.2)** box inline in this markdown file. Fill the Notes field for any FAIL or DEFERRED entry with the chosen D-08 routing option (hotfix-50.X / defer-to-v1.15.2 / re-test-pending). Sign and date the bottom block. Commit the signed state.

## Sign-off precondition

**Pass-or-Documented-Defer for ALL 10 entries is the precondition for Phase 51 / REL-04 / milestone close** (per REQUIREMENTS.md REL-04 + ROADMAP.md Phase 51 success criterion 5). Phase 51 release gate reads this file directly to verify all 10 boxes are checked Pass — or annotated DEFERRED with a documented D-08 route. A bare FAIL with no route blocks milestone close until resolved.

### SMOKE-02 — Notch-safe chrome (portrait)

**Closes:** NOTCH-01

**Setup:** Launch NICU Assist from the Home Screen icon in portrait orientation. Land on any calculator route (default `/morphine-wean`).

**Action:** Look at the title bar at the top of the screen.

**Expected:** Hamburger / wordmark / theme button visible below the Dynamic Island in portrait orientation — closes NOTCH-01.

**Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____

---

### SMOKE-03 — Drawer open without keyboard

**Closes:** FOCUS-01 + FOCUS-02

**Setup:** On `/morphine-wean` (or any calculator), with VoiceOver enabled (Settings → Accessibility → VoiceOver → On). Drawer is collapsed.

**Action:** Tap the "Tap to edit inputs" sticky drawer trigger to expand the drawer.

**Expected:** Drawer opens with no keyboard appearing, focus on close button, VoiceOver announces the drawer — closes FOCUS-01 + FOCUS-02.

**Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____

---

### SMOKE-04 — Drawer above keyboard

**Closes:** DRAWER-06 + DRAWER-07

**Setup:** On `/morphine-wean` with the drawer expanded, no keyboard up.

**Action:** Tap the Weight input field.

**Expected:** Tapping a weight field summons the keyboard AND the drawer is anchored above the keyboard top with ≥ 8 px clearance; first input + eyebrow remain visible — closes DRAWER-06 + DRAWER-07.

**Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____

---

### SMOKE-05 — Keyboard dismiss returns flush

**Closes:** DRAWER-02 (iOS 26 #800125 regression survival)

**Setup:** On `/morphine-wean` with the drawer expanded and the iOS soft keyboard summoned (continuing from SMOKE-04).

**Action:** Tap the iOS keyboard's "Done" button (or tap outside any input field) to dismiss the keyboard.

**Expected:** Dismissing the keyboard (Done button) returns the drawer smoothly to bottom-nav-top with `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` clearance; no flicker, no stale offset — closes DRAWER-02 (iOS 26 #800125 regression survival).

**Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____

---

### SMOKE-06 — bfcache restore renders flush

**Closes:** DRAWER-03

**Setup:** On `/morphine-wean` with the drawer expanded and at least one input populated. Tester picks ONE of the two reproductions below at the bedside (D-19/D-20 — both are equally valid):

- **Option A (Phone-app round-trip — PITFALLS.md P-04 worst case):** Swipe up to the App Switcher → open the Phone app → tap any recent contact's "FaceTime" tile (do NOT actually call) → swipe back to NICU Assist via the App Switcher.
- **Option B (Lock-then-wake — sleep/wake bfcache restore):** Press the side button to lock the device → wait 30 seconds → unlock with Face ID / passcode → return to NICU Assist by tapping the Home Screen icon.

**Action:** Observe the drawer state immediately on return — do NOT tap or scroll first.

**Expected:** Calling yourself / switching apps and returning renders the drawer flush without requiring a user gesture — closes DRAWER-03.

**Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes (record which option used: A / B): ____

---

### SMOKE-07 — Hardware keyboard does not lift drawer

**Closes:** DRAWER-09

**Setup:** Pair a Bluetooth keyboard (or attach Smart Connector keyboard) to the test iPhone. Confirm the keyboard is connected (Settings → Bluetooth → device shows Connected). Open NICU Assist from the Home Screen, navigate to `/morphine-wean`, expand the drawer.

**Hardware-keyboard escape hatch (per D-17/D-18):** If no Bluetooth/Smart Connector keyboard is available at smoke-time, annotate this entry as "DEFERRED — no hardware keyboard available" in the Notes field below and check the **DEFERRED** box. Routes per D-08 option 3 (re-test pending). This does NOT block overall sign-off — 9/10 Pass + 1 DEFERRED-with-route is acceptable for Phase 51 / REL-04.

**Action:** With the hardware keyboard connected, tap the Weight input field on screen.

**Expected:** Hardware-keyboard-paired iPhone does NOT lift the drawer — closes DRAWER-09.

**Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] / **DEFERRED:** [ ] — Notes: ____

### SMOKE-08 — Landscape inset respected

**Closes:** NOTCH-02

**Setup:** On any calculator route in portrait orientation.

**Action:** Rotate the iPhone to landscape (left or right). Observe the title bar chrome. Then rotate back to portrait and observe again.

**Expected:** Landscape rotation respects `safe-area-inset-left/right`; portrait re-rotation preserves notch-safe top — closes NOTCH-02.

**Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____

### SMOKE-09 — Light-mode status-bar legibility

**Closes:** PITFALLS.md `black-translucent` legibility gap (no upstream NOTCH/FOCUS/DRAWER ID — verifies existing `<meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="black-translucent">` in `src/app.html`)

**Setup:** Switch the app to light mode (theme toggle in title bar → light). Observe the iOS status bar at the top of the screen — clock, battery indicator, signal bars, Dynamic Island controls.

**Failure routing (per D-15/D-16):** If status-bar text is illegible against `var(--color-surface)` light value, this routes to **v1.15.2 — DEFER per D-08 option 2**. The mitigation is the FOUC-script-toggled `<meta name="theme-color">` (PITFALLS.md P-12) which requires non-trivial FOUC-script work and is out of v1.15.1 hotfix scope. Phase 50 only verifies whether the existing setup is acceptable — does NOT implement P-12. A FAIL here does NOT block v1.15.1 milestone close; it is documented as a v1.15.2 backlog item in PROJECT.md and the milestone ships without it.

**Action:** Read the time / battery percentage / Dynamic Island controls against the light-mode title-bar background.

**Expected:** Light mode + Dynamic Island shows status-bar text legible against `var(--color-surface)` light value (if poor, mitigation is light/dark `<meta name="theme-color">` toggled by the FOUC script) — closes the PITFALLS.md `black-translucent` legibility gap.

**Pass:** [ ] / **Fail (route to v1.15.2):** [ ] — Notes: ____

### SMOKE-10 — Cross-calculator parity

**Closes:** Cross-calculator parity for FOCUS-03 + DRAWER-05 (single-source-of-truth contract — drawer-only behavior across the 6 calculators per D-13, NOT a 60-cell sweep)

**Setup:** From the Home Screen icon in standalone PWA mode, in portrait orientation. The same drawer contract verified in SMOKE-03 (drawer-open-no-keyboard) + SMOKE-04 (drawer-above-keyboard) is exercised on each of the six calculator routes.

**Action:** For EACH of the six calculators below, navigate to the route, expand the drawer, then tap an input field. Tick Pass/Fail per route. Per D-12: 6 sub-bullets, one per calculator, drawer-only behavior — independent Pass/Fail boxes.

**Expected:** All six calculators (Morphine, Formula, GIR, Feeds, UAC/UVC, PERT) smoke-tested for drawer + notch behavior — no per-calculator divergence found, or any divergence captured as a follow-up todo.

  - [ ] **Morphine Wean** (`/morphine-wean`) — drawer-open-no-keyboard + drawer-above-keyboard parity. **Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____
  - [ ] **Formula** (`/formula`) — drawer-open-no-keyboard + drawer-above-keyboard parity. **Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____
  - [ ] **GIR** (`/gir`) — drawer-open-no-keyboard + drawer-above-keyboard parity. **Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____
  - [ ] **Feeds** (`/feeds`) — drawer-open-no-keyboard + drawer-above-keyboard parity. **Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____
  - [ ] **UAC/UVC** (`/uac-uvc`) — drawer-open-no-keyboard + drawer-above-keyboard parity. **Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____
  - [ ] **PERT** (`/pert`) — drawer-open-no-keyboard + drawer-above-keyboard parity. **Pass:** [ ] / **Fail:** [ ] — Notes: ____

## Failure Routing

If any SMOKE step fails, the tester chooses one of three follow-up actions at fail-time (not lock-time):

1. **Hotfix in v1.15.1** — open a decimal phase 50.X for the fix; the failing SMOKE step is re-tested after the fix lands; the gate stays open. Use this when the failure is small, fixable now, and blocks bedside use.
2. **Defer to v1.15.2 / v1.16** — capture as a backlog requirement; annotate the failing checklist line with the deferral target (e.g., "DEFERRED → v1.15.2 per P-12 mitigation"); the gate closes with the failure documented in PROJECT.md. Use this when the failure routes to known mitigation work that is out of v1.15.1 scope (e.g., SMOKE-09 → v1.15.2 per D-15/D-16).
3. **Re-test on different iPhone / iOS version** — annotate the line "RE-TEST PENDING" and re-run on a different device before deciding option 1 vs option 2. Use this when a likely device-specific regression needs reproduction first.

Failure routing is the tester's judgment call at the bedside. Phase 50 plan does NOT pre-commit to which fails route to which action.
